abstract.t: The document presents an optional new type of database synchronization packet called an Aggregated SNP Hash (ASH). When feasible, it compresses traditional SNP exchanges into a dynamic Merkle tree-like structure, which speeds up synchronization of large databases and adjacency numbers while reducing the load from regular CSNP exchanges during normal operation. Just like CSNPs and PSNPs, ASH packets come in two flavors, called Complete ASH (CASH) and Partial ASH (PASH).
